meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Content Creation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Master debugging by documenting your process. A premium case study for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate technical growth.
content.answer_block: When you hit a wall, stop staring at the screen. Pivot to content creation by documenting your debugging journey. Use Antigravity to analyze your logic and Claude Code to automate the refactoring process. By externalizing your problem-solving, you turn technical roadblocks into high-value portfolio content while accelerating your learning.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking in rhythm with your mounting frustration. That elusive bug has survived three hours of Stack Overflow deep-dives and frantic documentation reading. You feel like an imposter, convinced that 'real' developers just know how to fix this instinctively. 

But here is the secret: the best engineers aren't just coders; they are masters of articulation. By shifting your mindset from 'fix the code' to 'document the process,' you transform a moment of technical failure into a masterclass in engineering communication. This is how you build authority while you learn to build software.

steps.title: Define the Failure State
steps.description: Articulate exactly what the code is doing versus what it should do. This forces clarity before you start typing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior mentor. I have a bug in this component. Here is the behavior: [Insert Description]. Analyze why my mental model of this logic is incorrect.

steps.title: Generate the Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use AI to dissect the specific failure point, ensuring you understand the 'why' behind the bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a Root Cause Analysis on this code block. Identify the edge case I missed and explain the architectural flaw.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze this function for race conditions and state management errors'

steps.title: Draft the Technical Narrative
steps.description: Write a 'Learning Journal' entry. Explain the bug to a peer to solidify your own understanding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Turn my debugging notes into a concise, technical blog post structure. Emphasize the 'Aha!' moment of finding the bug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --prompt 'Draft a 300-word technical post based on these logs'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix using Claude Code to ensure the implementation follows industry best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a refactor that prevents this specific bug from recurring. Focus on clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --file <filename> --issue 'refactor to handle null pointers'

steps.title: Final Verification & Publication
steps.description: Validate the fix and prepare your content for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my final implementation. Does it meet production standards? If not, what is missing?

pro_tips: Always treat your commit message as a mini-blog post explaining the 'why' of the change.
Use Claude Code's history logs to recreate your debugging steps in your documentation.
If you can't explain the bug to a rubber duck, you don't understand the bug yet; keep debugging.
Focus on the 'Trade-offs' section of your documentation to show senior-level design thinking.
Keep a 'Bug Library' repository where you document every major blocker you've solved for future reference.
content.next_steps: Take your documented debugging session and post it on Dev.to or your personal portfolio site. Then, pick one more bug from your current project and repeat the process to build your content pipeline.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs stall your progress. Turn your hurdles into your competitive advantage. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
